_schema.description: Spoken command phrases ('verbal commands') the ServUO server reacts to. Most are SPEECH KEYWORDS: the client matches your (localized) phrase against its speech-keyword table and sends a numeric keyword id (keyword_id) in the encoded-speech packet; server handlers test e.HasKeyword(0x..). Because the id is resolved client-side from the localized table, the equivalent phrase in any client language triggers the same action — the server only sees the id, not the words. A few commands (match_type 'literal-string') compare the raw text and must be said in English exactly. 'phrase' is the canonical English wording; for keyword commands it is what an English client maps to that id. <...> denotes a value you supply (a pet/vendor/NPC name or an amount). Server reply text resolved via anima cliloc.json where available.

match_types.keyword: matched by client-resolved speech keyword id (e.HasKeyword); language-independent.
match_types.literal-string: matched against the raw text (Insensitive.Equals/StartsWith/IndexOf); must be said in English exactly.

Housing.phrase: I wish to lock this down
Housing.effect: Prompts you to target a loose item to lock it down (fixes it in place, stops decay). Must be a friend or better, inside the house. Server prompt: "Lock what down?" (cliloc 502097).
Prompts you to target a locked-down item to release it back to loose status so it can be moved/picked up. Server prompt: "Choose the item you wish to release" (cliloc 502100).
Prompts you to target a container to make it secure storage (access-controlled). Co-owner or better. Server prompt: "Choose the item you wish to secure" (cliloc 502103).
Prompts you to target a secured container to unsecure it. Owner only. Server prompt: "Choose the item you wish to unsecure" (cliloc 502106).

Housing.phrase: I wish to place a strongbox
Housing.effect: A co-owner gets a personal strongbox placed; owners are told they do not get one of their own (502109). Server prompt: "Owners do not get a strongbox of their own." (cliloc 502109).

Housing.phrase: I wish to place a trash barrel
Housing.effect: Places a trash barrel in the house (items dropped in it are destroyed). Co-owner or better.

Prompts you to target a person to ban them from the house (public houses only; on a private AOS house you revoke access instead). Friend or better. Server prompt: "Target the individual to ban from this house." (cliloc 501325).
Prompts you to target a person to eject (kick) them from the house without banning. Friend or better. Server prompt: "Target the individual to eject from this house." (cliloc 501326).

Housing.phrase: I wish to resize my house
Housing.effect: Opens the resize/redemolish confirmation gump for the owner (Mondain's Legacy+). Matched as a literal English string, not a keyword, so it only works said exactly in English; you must be at the house sign and the house must be older than one hour.

Pets.effect: Every controlled pet in earshot attacks a target you then pick. (0x169 = 'all attack' fires the same handler.)
Every pet goes into guard mode, guarding you. (0x16B = 'all guard me'.)
Every pet follows a target you then pick.
Every pet stops its current order (idle).
Named pet attacks a target you pick. Owner only; name required.
Named pet guards. Owner only; name required.
Named pet follows a target you pick ('follow') or follows you ('follow me', 0x163).
Named pet comes to you. Owner only.
Named pet stops its current order.
Named pet patrols its home area. Owner only.
Named pet drops what it is carrying (pack animals). Owner only; not for summons/dead pets.
Prompts you to target a player to add as a pet-friend (they can command it too). Owner only; not for summons.
Prompts you to target a player to transfer ownership of the pet to them. Owner only; not for summons/dead pets.
Releases the pet from your control (opens a confirm gump for tamed pets; summons are dismissed immediately). Owner only.
Game Master only: forces the named creature to accept you as its control master. Matched as the literal word 'obey' after the creature's name.

Vendors/Bank.effect: The banker tells you your current gold balance. (e.g. "Thy current bank balance is ~1_AMOUNT~ gold.")
Withdraws that much gold from your bank to your backpack (e.g. "withdraw 1000"). The amount is parsed from the words after the keyword.
The banker writes you a bank check for that amount, drawn from your bank balance.
Opens the shopkeeper's sell window so you can sell goods.
Opens the buy window of the named shopkeeper ('buy' alone works when you name them).
Opens the sell window of the named shopkeeper ('sell' alone works when you name them).
The animal trainer offers to stable a pet (target the pet).
The animal trainer brings out your stabled pets, or claim a specific one by saying its name after 'claim'.
On a player vendor: opens its for-sale list to buy items.
Browse a player vendor's stock without buying.
Owner: collect the gold the vendor has earned.
Owner: check the vendor's fees/funds status.

Misc.effect: The server reports your short-term and long-term murder counts.

Misc.phrase: I renounce my young player status
Misc.effect: Opens the confirmation to give up Young-player protection.
Opens your guild info window (if you are in a guild).
Calls the town guards to your location (in a guarded region).
The town crier (or a news-reading object like Coral the Owl / Sherry the Mouse) recites the current news. Works within ~12 tiles.
Said (with the guildmaster named) to an NPC guildmaster to ask to join their guild.
Said to your NPC guildmaster to resign from the guild.
A real-estate broker asks you to target a house deed and appraises its value. Reply: "Which deed would you like appraised?".
An escortable NPC tells you where it wants to go.
Accept the escort quest for an escortable NPC.
Ask the Thieves' Guildmaster about a disguise kit (members only).
Asks a hireable NPC to work for you; it quotes its daily wage.
A town sheriff issues orders to a faction guard (sheriff only).
Asks a townsperson NPC what skills they can teach you; '<npc name> <skillname>' then trains that skill a little.
Asks an NPC for the in-game time.
